headlines.title: Magnitude 7.4 Earthquake Topples Buildings in Northern Indonesia, Kills 1 Person
headlines.summary: Undersea 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck off Indonesia on April 1, toppling buildings in Manado, killing at least one person and generating 75cm tsunami waves. Thousands fled homes; affects regional stability amid seismic activity. Monitoring continues for aftershocks and damage assessments.

headlines.title: NASA Launches Artemis II with Four Astronauts on First Crewed Moon Mission in 50 Years
headlines.summary: Artemis II crew of three Americans and one Canadian lifted off from Kennedy Space Center on April 1, beginning 10-day lunar flyby. Marks restart of human Moon exploration toward south pole landings. Boosts global space ambitions and technology sharing.

headlines.title: Israeli Air Defences Intercept Three Waves of Iranian Missiles Near Tel Aviv
headlines.summary: Israel intercepted multiple Iranian missile barrages early April 2, lightly wounding several in Tel Aviv area after US presidential address. Triggers sirens across country; escalates regional conflict, raises escalation fears. Live updates track further attacks.

headlines.title: Trump Delivers Primetime Address on Iran War Progress and Bombing Threats
headlines.summary: President Trump claimed US nears goals in Iran war during April 1 speech, promising to bomb Iran back to Stone Age. Affects global markets and alliances; signals intensified military push amid ongoing conflict. Viewers await policy shifts.

headlines.title: US Lifts Sanctions on Venezuela Acting President Delcy Rodríguez
headlines.summary: US removes sanctions on interim leader Delcy Rodríguez after capturing Nicolás Maduro, recognizing her authority. Eases economic pressures on Venezuela; affects oil markets and regional stability. Further relief measures possible amid transition.

framings.angle: Emphasizes US recognition of Rodríguez as legitimate post-Maduro capture
Highlights gradual US easing of Venezuela sanctions after toppling Maduro
Notes sanctions relief as latest recognition of Rodríguez since Maduro capture

headlines.title: Appeals Court Rejects HUD Plan to Shift Homelessness Funding to Sobriety Programs
headlines.summary: Federal appeals court blocks HUD overhaul redirecting funds from permanent housing to conditional programs. Advocates say it risks increasing homelessness; affects 600,000 unsheltered Americans. HUD must maintain current priorities.

headlines.title: FDA Approves Daily Obesity Pill Foundayo to Compete with Wegovy
headlines.summary: FDA fast-tracks Foundayo pill as non-injection option for weight loss, targeting millions with obesity. Competes with Wegovy pill form; expands access to treatments reducing health risks like diabetes. Rollout begins soon.

headlines.title: UK Hosts 30 Countries to Plan Reopening of Strait of Hormuz Shipping Route
headlines.summary: Britain chairs virtual meeting of 35 nations on April 3 to pressure reopening Hormuz strait choked by US-Israel-Iran war. Affects 20% global oil trade; impacts energy prices worldwide. Diplomatic efforts seek de-escalation.

headlines.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Overrun Troops and Enter Goma Displacing One Million
headlines.summary: Rebels entered Congo safe-haven city Goma amid gunfire, shelling; UN estimates over 1 million displaced inside. Overruns troops; threatens aid hub, worsens humanitarian crisis for millions. Evacuations and reinforcements underway.
headlines.previousContext: Rebels advanced into Goma outskirts yesterday; today they enter city center with active combat displacing 1 million residents

headlines.title: Palestinians Stream into Northern Gaza After Israel Breaks Hostage Deadlock
headlines.summary: Displaced Gazans cross military zone on foot into north after hostage release impasse resolved. Affects hundreds of thousands returning; eases division of enclave. Further movements depend on security and aid access.
headlines.previousContext: Initial crossings occurred yesterday post-hostage deal; today large streams return following deadlock resolution

headlines.title: Republicans Plan DHS Funding Vote to End Record Partial Government Shutdown
headlines.summary: Senate tests Republican measure Thursday to fund most DHS through September, excluding immigration enforcement. Addresses longest shutdown; affects 200,000 employees. House follow-up aims for three-year funding without Democrats.
headlines.previousContext: Pressure mounted yesterday; today deal announced with Thursday test vote for partial funding resolution
